description: Papers on micro-randomized trial methodology from 2020-2026, covering WCLS estimation, causal excursion effects, power analysis, sample size, efficiency gains, missing data, non-stationarity, and hybrid designs.

papers.label: The Micro-Randomized Trial for Developing Digital Interventions: Data Analysis Methods
papers.authors: Tianchen Qian, Michael A. Russell, Linda M. Collins, Predrag Klasnja, Stephanie T. Lanza, Hyesun Yoo, Susan A. Murphy
papers.headline: Introduced the WCLS estimator for causal excursion effects in MRTs with worked HeartSteps examples
papers.detail: Provides formal definition of causal excursion effects for MRT data analysis and introduces the weighted and centered least-squares (WCLS) estimator. Covers primary and secondary analyses with SAS/R implementation. Uses HeartSteps MRT as running example throughout.
papers.notes: Foundational MRT methods paper; defines causal excursion effects and WCLS estimator used across all subsequent MRT analyses.

papers.label: Multi-Level Micro-Randomized Trial: Detecting the Proximal Effect of Messages on Physical Activity
papers.authors: Jing Xu, Xiaoxi Yan, Caroline Figueroa, Joseph Jay Williams, Bibhas Chakraborty
papers.headline: Extended MRT design to multi-level intervention components with GEE-based sample size calculators
papers.detail: Proposes multi-level MRT (MLMRT) design allowing more than two levels per intervention component. Develops GEE-based test statistics for proximal effects and associated sample size calculators. Motivated by DIAMANTE smartphone study for physical activity in diabetes and mental health. R Shiny app provided.
papers.notes: First paper to extend MRT framework beyond binary treatments; R Shiny app for sample size calculation.

papers.label: Assessing Time-Varying Causal Effect Moderation in the Presence of Cluster-Level Treatment Effect Heterogeneity
papers.authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Extended causal excursion effects to cluster-level heterogeneity and interference in MRTs
papers.detail: Revisits causal excursion effects under potential cluster-level treatment effect heterogeneity and interference. Tests moderation by cluster-level variables using data from multi-institution Intern Health Study MRT of first-year medical residents.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Intern Health Study analysis
papers.notes: First MRT paper to relax between-subject independence and non-interference assumptions.

papers.label: The Micro-Randomized Trial for Developing Digital Interventions: Experimental Design and Data Analysis Considerations
papers.authors: Tianchen Qian, Ashley E. Walton, Linda M. Collins, Predrag Klasnja, Stephanie T. Lanza, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Mashifiqui Rabbi, Michael A. Russell, Maureen A. Walton, Hyesun Yoo, Susan A. Murphy
papers.headline: Comprehensive tutorial on why, when, and how to use MRTs for JITAI development
papers.detail: Comprehensive review covering MRT planning, design, implementation, primary and secondary analyses. Covers causal excursion effects, WCLS estimator, and software implementation. Uses HeartSteps, SARA, and BariFit MRTs as illustrations. Clarifies design considerations for researchers new to MRTs.
papers.notes: Definitive tutorial for practitioners; overlaps with arXiv:2004.10241 and arXiv:2005.05880.

papers.label: Estimating Time-Varying Direct and Indirect Causal Excursion Effects with Longitudinal Binary Outcomes
papers.authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Extended MRT causal framework to decompose direct and indirect effects through time-varying mediators
papers.detail: Develops methodology for decomposing total causal excursion effects into direct and indirect effects in MRTs with binary outcomes. Addresses time-varying mediation in mobile health intervention settings where intermediate variables may be affected by treatment.
papers.notes: Bridges causal mediation analysis with the MRT causal excursion effect framework.

papers.label: A Meta-Learning Method for Estimation of Causal Excursion Effects to Assess Time-Varying Moderation
papers.authors: Jieru Shi, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Proposed doubly robust DR-WCLS estimator handling unknown randomization, missing data, and high-dimensional features
papers.detail: Proposes DR-WCLS, a flexible doubly robust inferential procedure for causal excursion effects from a meta-learner perspective. Handles uncertain randomization probabilities, missing observations, and high-dimensional mHealth data. Demonstrates consistent and more efficient estimates than existing WCLS methods.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Intern Health Study analysis
papers.notes: Key advance: DR-WCLS relaxes three major WCLS assumptions simultaneously.

papers.label: Incorporating Auxiliary Variables to Improve the Efficiency of Time-Varying Treatment Effect Estimation
papers.authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Showed auxiliary variable adjustment improves MRT estimation efficiency with local efficiency guarantees
papers.detail: Proposes method to incorporate auxiliary variables beyond prespecified moderators into MRT estimation to reduce chance imbalances and improve asymptotic efficiency. Extends to post-treatment auxiliary variables for lagged treatment effects. Demonstrated on Intern Health Study data.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Intern Health Study analysis
papers.notes: Addresses practical issue of underutilized sensor data in MRT analyses.

papers.label: Estimating Causal Effects for Binary Outcomes Using Per-Decision Inverse Probability Weighting
papers.authors: Yihan Bao, Lauren Bell, Elizabeth Williamson, Claire Garnett, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Reduced IPW variance in MRT binary outcomes by 2x+ using per-decision IPW
papers.detail: Proposes per-decision IPW estimators that avoid variance inflation from product weighting when later treatments occur within the outcome window. Second estimator adds semiparametric efficiency projection. Demonstrated substantial efficiency improvement over standard IPW.
papers.notes: Key practical advance for binary proximal outcomes common in mHealth (e.g., app opens).

papers.label: Incorporating nonparametric methods for estimating causal excursion effects in mobile health with zero-inflated count outcomes
papers.authors: Xueqing Liu, Tianchen Qian, Lauren Bell, Bibhas Chakraborty
papers.headline: Extended causal excursion effects to zero-inflated count outcomes with nonparametric estimation
papers.detail: Develops estimation approaches for causal excursion effects with zero-inflated count proximal outcomes. Incorporates nonparametric techniques with bidirectional asymptotics. Motivated by and applied to Drink Less MRT for alcohol reduction.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Drink Less MRT analysis
papers.notes: Addresses a common but previously unaddressed outcome type in MRTs: zero-inflated counts.

papers.label: Efficient and Globally Robust Causal Excursion Effect Estimation
papers.authors: Zhaoxi Cheng, Lauren Bell, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Derived semiparametric efficiency bound for CEE and proposed efficient globally robust estimators
papers.detail: Derives semiparametric efficiency bound for causal excursion effects with identity/log links. Proposes two-stage estimators achieving the efficiency bound and robust to misspecified nuisance models. Establishes general theory for globally robust Z-estimators. Demonstrated substantial efficiency gains on Drink Less MRT data.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Drink Less MRT analysis
papers.notes: Theoretical ceiling: derives the fundamental efficiency bound for CEE estimation.

papers.label: Data integration methods for micro-randomized trials
papers.authors: Easton Huch, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Lindsey Potter, Cho Lam, David W. Wetter, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Developed meta-analytic data integration combining multiple MRTs for efficiency gains
papers.detail: Develops methods to integrate data from multiple related MRTs to improve statistical efficiency. Uses multivariate precision-weighted meta-estimator with asymptotic optimality. Applied to two smoking cessation MRTs.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with two smoking cessation MRTs
papers.effect_size: N/A - methodological; efficiency gains from data integration
papers.notes: Published in Biometrics 2025. First paper to formally address MRT data integration.

papers.label: Modeling Time-Varying Effects of Mobile Health Interventions Using Longitudinal Functional Data from HeartSteps Micro-Randomized Trial
papers.authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: First semiparametric varying-coefficient model for time-varying MRT effects using functional outcomes
papers.detail: Proposes first semiparametric causal excursion effect model with varying coefficients for time-varying effects within and across decision points. Uses double time indices for longitudinal functional step count outcomes. HeartSteps analysis shows prompt effects vary by context (sedentary, weekday, suggestion type).
papers.effect_size: Varies by context; prompts more effective when recently sedentary
papers.notes: Directly relevant to project context: HeartSteps analysis with granular step count outcomes.

papers.label: Doubly Robust Estimation of Causal Excursion Effects in Micro-Randomized Trials with Missing Longitudinal Outcomes
papers.authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: First doubly robust CEE estimator handling MAR missing data in MRTs
papers.detail: Two-stage doubly robust estimator for causal excursion effects when longitudinal outcomes are missing at random. Handles continuous, binary, and count outcomes. Consistent and asymptotically normal if either missingness or outcome model is correct. Demonstrated on HeartSteps data.
papers.notes: Addresses missing data problem pervasive in mHealth MRTs (sensor non-wear, missed self-reports).

papers.label: Evaluation of the HeartSteps Online Sampling Algorithm
papers.authors: Xiang Meng, Walter Dempsey, Peng Liao, Nick Reid, Pedja Klasnja, Susan Murphy
papers.headline: HeartSteps V2V3 online randomization algorithm successfully met intervention frequency and uniformity constraints
papers.detail: Case study evaluating the online sampling algorithm used in HeartSteps V2V3 MRT to manage two constraints: average 1.5 interventions/day and uniform delivery across decision times. Algorithm performed well on both metrics. Provides practical recommendations for constrained MRT randomization.
papers.notes: Directly relevant to HeartSteps constraint handling; important for power-constrained exploration in MRTs.

papers.label: Distal Causal Excursion Effects: Modeling Long-Term Effects of Time-Varying Treatments in Micro-Randomized Trials
papers.authors: Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Introduced distal causal excursion effects (DCEE) for long-term behavior change in MRTs
papers.detail: Novel estimand DCEE quantifies long-term effects of time-varying treatments on distal outcomes. Contrasts outcomes under two excursion policies while marginalizing over most treatment assignments. HeartSteps analysis: prompts delivered earlier in the study had stronger long-term habit formation effects.
papers.effect_size: Earlier prompts had stronger long-term effects on habit formation
papers.notes: Critical for project: bridges MRT proximal effects to long-term behavior change outcomes.

papers.label: Micro-randomized Trials with Categorical Treatments: Causal Effect Estimation and Sample Size Calculation
papers.authors: Jeremy Lin, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Extended WCLS and sample size formulas to multi-categorical MRT treatments
papers.detail: Extends causal excursion effect definition and WCLS estimator to MRTs with categorical (3+) treatment levels. Develops sample size formula for comparing categorical treatment levels with type I error and power guarantees. Provides practical guidelines. Illustrated on HeartSteps.
papers.notes: Directly relevant: 4-action MDP requires multi-categorical treatment methods.

papers.label: Semiparametric Off-Policy Inference for Optimal Policy Values under Possible Non-Uniqueness
papers.authors: Haoyu Wei
papers.headline: Developed NSAVE method for off-policy inference with Drink Less MRT application
papers.detail: Studies inference for optimal policy values in MDPs. Proposes Nonparametric Sequential Value Evaluation (NSAVE) method yielding martingale-based inference with double-robustness. Applies to Drink Less MRT for confidence intervals on state-adaptive notification policies.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Drink Less MRT analysis
papers.notes: Relevant for off-policy evaluation when transitioning from MRT to RL-based policies.

papers.label: Dynamic Causal Mediation Analysis for Intensive Longitudinal Data
papers.authors: Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Introduced natural direct/indirect excursion effects for mediation in MRTs
papers.detail: Proposes natural direct and indirect excursion effects to decompose total excursion effects in MRTs. Derives efficient influence functions and multiply-robust estimators. Applied to HeartSteps MRT and SleepHealth observational study.
papers.notes: Bridges MRT causal inference with mediation analysis for understanding why interventions work.

papers.label: Robust Estimation of Moderated Causal Excursion Odds Ratio in Micro-Randomized Trials
papers.authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Doubly robust estimators for moderated causal excursion odds ratio in MRTs
papers.detail: Two estimators for moderated causal excursion odds ratio for binary outcomes. First is doubly robust when moderator captures sequential randomization; second handles general settings with robustness to association model misspecification. Applied to Drink Less MRT.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Drink Less MRT analysis
papers.notes: Provides odds-ratio scale alternative to relative risk for binary proximal outcomes.

papers.label: Doubly Robust Estimation with Stabilized Weights for Binary Proximal Outcomes in Micro-Randomized Trials
papers.authors: Jinho Cha, Eunchan Cha
papers.headline: DR-EMEE achieves 2x efficiency gain over IPW and 5-10% over EMEE for binary MRT outcomes
papers.detail: Proposes doubly robust EMEE with stabilized and truncated weights for binary proximal outcomes. Proves double robustness, asymptotic efficiency. Up to twofold efficiency gains over IPW. Validated on HeartSteps, PAMAP2, and mHealth datasets.
papers.effect_size: 2x efficiency gain over IPW, 5-10% over EMEE
papers.notes: Practical efficiency gains for binary outcomes common in mHealth engagement metrics.

papers.label: Power Constrained Nonstationary Bandits with Habituation and Recovery Dynamics
papers.authors: Fengxu Li, Stephanie M. Carpenter, Matthew P. Buman, Yonatan Mintz
papers.headline: Thompson Sampling with probability clipping balances personalization and power in MRTs
papers.detail: Develops ROGUE-TS for nonstationary bandits with habituation/recovery dynamics. Introduces probability clipping to balance personalization and population-level learning. Validated on two MRT datasets (physical activity, bipolar disorder). Maintains statistical power while lowering regret.
papers.effect_size: Lower regret than baselines; maintains power via clipping
papers.notes: Directly relevant to power-constrained exploration. Bridges bandit algorithms and MRT statistical validity.

papers.label: Prediction Intervals for Individual Treatment Effects in a Multiple Decision Point Framework using Conformal Inference
papers.authors: Swaraj Bose, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Conformal prediction intervals for time-varying ITEs in MRTs with non-exchangeability guarantees
papers.detail: Proposes conformal inference method for prediction intervals of time-varying individual treatment effects. Weaker exchangeability assumptions. Coverage guarantee dependent on non-exchangeability degree. Applied to Intern Health Study MRT.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Intern Health Study analysis
papers.notes: Extends MRT inference from average to individual-level effects with uncertainty quantification.

papers.label: Evaluating time-varying treatment effects in hybrid SMART-MRT designs
papers.authors: Mengbing Li, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: First causal estimands and analysis methods for hybrid SMART+MRT designs
papers.detail: Formally defines synergistic effects in hybrid SMART-MRT designs integrating human-delivered and digital components at different timescales. Assesses proximal and distal outcomes. Applied to M-Bridge targeting binge drinking reduction in college students.
papers.notes: Relevant for multi-timescale adaptation combining coach and digital components.

papers.label: Estimation of Time-Varying Treatment Effects in a Joint Model for Longitudinal and Recurrent Event Outcomes
papers.authors: Madeline R Abbott, Jeremy M G Taylor, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Lindsey N Potter, David W Wetter, Cho Y Lam, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Bayesian joint model handles MRT treatment effects on longitudinal and recurrent event outcomes
papers.detail: Model-based approach for MRT treatment effect estimation via joint longitudinal-survival model. Bayesian inference with information criteria. Demonstrated on MRT of substance use with multiple outcomes and recurrent events.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with substance use MRT data
papers.notes: Joint modeling approach for complex MRT outcomes beyond simple proximal responses.
